mnemonics.title: CFG to PDA Conversion Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: GOTO (Get Output To Operate): When converting CFG to PDA, use the 'GOTO' rule - For each production A→α, push α on stack. Start with start symbol S on stack, accept by empty stack.

mnemonics.title: Push and Pop PDA
mnemonics.content: PUSH-A: Productions become 'push' operations. POP-P: Terminals cause 'pop' operations. ACCEPT when STACK empties completely.

mnemonics.title: Empty Stack vs Final State
mnemonics.content: E.S.F.A. = Empty Stack Final Acceptance: PDAε accepts by empty stack. PDAF accepts by final state. Both are equivalent in power.

mnemonics.title: CFG PDA Equivalence
mnemonics.content: CFG = Grammar that GENERATES. PDA = Automaton that RECOGNIZES. They are EQUAL in power - every CFG has an equivalent PDA and vice versa.

mnemonics.title: CNF GNF Shortcut
mnemonics.content: CNF: Chomsky Normal Form - Only A→BC or A→a. GNF: Greibach Normal Form - All productions start with terminal. CFG → CNF → GNF simplifies PDA construction.

acronyms.usage: A grammar where left side has single non-terminal; generates context-free languages. Used when writing productions like A→aBc
Finite automaton with infinite stack memory. Used to recognize context-free languages. Has states, transitions, stack alphabet
CFG in restricted form: A→BC or A→a only. Used to prove CFG-PDA equivalence and for CYK parsing algorithm
CFG where all productions are A→aα (terminal followed by terminals/non-terminals). Used for deterministic PDA construction
Represents empty string (λ). Used in productions like A→ε for nullable variables, and in PDA transitions
Accepts when stack becomes completely empty after reading input. Most common conversion from CFG
Accepts when reaches a designated final state after reading input (may have symbols in stack)

visualTips.concept: PDA Stack as a Plate Stack
visualTips.visualization: Imagine a restaurant plate dispenser: When you PUSH, you add a plate on top. When you POP, you remove the top plate. The PDA reads input and manages plates - terminal matched = pop that plate. Stack never empty = language rejected.
Draw 3-column table: Left=Production (A→BCD), Middle=PDA push (push B,C,D), Right=PDA pop (when reading b, pop B). Visualize stack growing DOWN (top at bottom of visual)
Root = Start symbol (on stack). Expand non-terminal = push RHS to stack. Match terminal = pop from stack. Bottom-up: Preorder traversal of parse tree equals PDA operations.
Draw a two-headed arrow: CFG ←→ PDA. Label left 'Generate' with grammar icon. Label right 'Recognize' with automaton icon. Both point to 'Language' bubble in center.
Draw stack as a container. Acceptance = container completely empty. Rejection = container still has items or input remains unread. Final state acceptance = checkmark on state, ignore stack.

analogies.analogy: CFG is like a recipe book with cooking instructions. The grammar rules (productions) tell you HOW to make each dish (generate string). PDA is like a chef who follows the recipe - reads ingredients (input) and prepares dish (accepts/rejects). The recipe book (CFG) and chef (PDA) both can create the same meals (recognize same language).

PDA stack is like a function call stack in programming. When you encounter a non-terminal (function call), you PUSH return address and parameters (production RHS) onto stack. When you read terminal (complete task), you POP. This tracks nested structure just like programming languages need for matching brackets/procedures.

Think of PDA as a person doing recursive descent parsing manually. For input 'aabb', start with S→aSb | ε. Read 'a', choose S→aSb, push S. Read 'a', choose again. When reading 'b', must match top of stack. The stack remembers which production to apply next - it's your 'to-do list'.

Converting CFG to PDA is like a compiler's parser generator (YACC/Bison). Grammar (CFG) gets converted to parsing tables (PDA). The parser (PDA) takes source code (input string) and uses stack to track grammar structure. Both grammar and parser accept/reject same programs.

analogies.concept: Empty Stack vs Final State as Exit Conditions
analogies.analogy: Empty stack acceptance is like leaving a mall only when you've completely finished shopping and empty cart. Final state acceptance is like leaving mall whenever you reach exit door (may still have items in cart). Both ways ensure you completed shopping, just different exit criteria. Same language recognized.
